objective: Generic protected operational lifecycle transitions: 14 ops over the canonized chain states via proposal/decision contracts with closed authority-effect objects, lifecycle-state envelope, a parameterized transitions route pair over the sealed-intent machinery, and an unsigned census epoch.
falsifiable_claim: No op admits an illegal predecessor status; no scope authorizes another op; a decision cannot approve a terminal effect the proposal did not declare; every transition admits exactly once with exact predecessor roots and reconstructs after restart.
adversarial_or_fault_proof: Committed: 6/6 canon-parity tests on the op family (row-by-row legality pinning, scope disjointness, degraded unreachable, terminal arcs) + 12/12 compile-path tests (full 154-cell op-by-predecessor sweep compiles or refuses per canon; identity detachment/overclaims refused; bootstrap and reserved statuses refused; byte-determinism with tamper-sensitive commitments; six negative claims preserved). ioi-types 262/262. Increment 3: three registered contracts embedding the closed effect; full architecture-contract bar green (39 contracts/141 fixtures incl. mutation+differential corpora; generated cargo tests 14/14 with the live ajv-vs-Rust differential); ten real-hash fixtures prove Rust/Python/JS recipe agreement; state root aligned to the flat JCS house pattern. Predeclared remaining: sealed-intent replay/restart convergence and fault-injection recovery in the runtime increment.
remaining_nonclaims: Amendment execution, migration/succession, dissolution, and enrollment remain named-owner slices (m1-5c/d).
No membership/topology (M2), consensus, or product Systems UX beyond pulled read projections.
degraded is an observed posture, never an op target.
source_provenance: Phased from work item m1-5-protected-transitions (internal groundwork); canon + op family committed on feat/system-lifecycle-transition-contracts.
